title: Caloglossum flabellatum
taxonomy: Caloglossum flabellatum is classified as a species of red algae in the family Chaetophoraceae. It belongs to the genus Caloglossum, which is a group of non-marine algae found in freshwater environments. Caloglossum flabellatum is typically classified as a member of the phylum Rhodophyta, which includes all red algae. The species is further categorized as a member of the class Florideophyceae. Caloglossum flabellatum's classification is part of the broader understanding of the algae kingdom's diversity and characteristics.
uses: Caloglossum flabellatum is a type of green alga, commonly used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The alga is rich in nutrients and has anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Caloglossum flabellatum has also been used as a remedy for digestive problems and wounds. Further research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits and confirm its efficacy.

side_effects: Caloglossum flabellatum is a type of green algae, not typically used as a medicinal herb. However, algae in general may cause gastrointestinal upset if ingested in large quantities. In rare cases, consuming algae can also lead to the accumulation of toxins such as microcystin. Additionally, algae can cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals. It's essential to exercise caution when handling or consuming algae-based products.
